titleStmt.title: Letter from Fergus Quinn to the Under Secretary for Ireland, 31 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Fergus Quinn

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Fergus Quinn (1861-1952) to the under secretary for Ireland, applying for three weeks’ leave.Fergus Quinn joined the Dublin Metropolitan Police (DMP) in 1881 and served until his retirement in 1920. In 1916 he was serving as the Assistant Commissioner for the DMP.A the time this letter was written Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) was serving as the under secretary for Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as the replacement under secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13127

correspAction.placeName: Metropolitan Police Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Under Secretary Annual Leave I beg that
three weeks' leave
be granted to me
commencing on
tomorrow 1st Aug 1916. Fergus Quinn
Asst Comms
